title: aesculus × carnea var. briotii
intro: Aesculus × carnea var. briotii, also known as the Red Horse Chestnut or Briotti's Red Horse Chestnut, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the Hippocastanaceae family. This plant is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that can help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Aesculus × carnea var. briotii include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting potential antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of infusions, tinctures, and salves, although it is essential to note that its use should be guided by a healthcare professional or experienced herbalist.
